turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Hi, doctor. I’m here for my blood pressure follow-up. I’ve also been getting headaches now and then.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Hello. Thanks for coming in. Let’s talk through both. How often are the headaches happening, and where do you feel them?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Maybe once or twice a week. It’s usually across my forehead or at the back of my head. Not very severe, but annoying.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Do they come with blurred vision, nausea, weakness, chest pain, or shortness of breath?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, none of that. Just the headache. Sometimes I feel a bit tense at work, so I thought it may be stress.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That’s helpful. Have you checked your blood pressure at home, or had readings done elsewhere recently?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I checked it at the pharmacy twice. It was around 145 over 90 on one day, and 138 over 88 on another.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Okay. And are you taking your amlodipine every day? Any missed doses or side effects like ankle swelling, flushing, or dizziness?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I do take it daily. I’ve missed one or two doses over the months, but not often. No swelling or anything like that.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Good. Any changes in your eyesight, headaches waking you from sleep, fever, neck stiffness, or head injury?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, nothing like that. The headaches don’t wake me up. I just notice them after a long day.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: All right. How are things with sleep, caffeine, alcohol on weekends, and stress at work?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Sleep is okay, but some weeks are busy. I do drink socially on weekends, maybe a few glasses. I have coffee most mornings.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: And exercise-wise, are you managing the walking you mentioned?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: A little, yes. I’m mostly sitting at work, but I try to walk a few times a week. Not as much as I should, honestly.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That’s still a good start. Any family history of high blood pressure, diabetes, stroke, or heart disease?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Yes, my mother has hypertension. My father had type 2 diabetes.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Thanks. From what you’ve told me, this sounds most likely like mildly elevated blood pressure with tension-type headaches, rather than anything dangerous. I’d like to check your blood pressure here today, review your readings, and decide if we should adjust your treatment. We should also keep an eye on lifestyle factors like salt intake, regular walking, caffeine, and stress. If your pressure is still running high, we may consider increasing the amlodipine or adding another medicine. Please seek urgent care if you develop severe sudden headache, chest pain, shortness of breath, weakness, confusion, or visual changes. Otherwise, let’s do the reading now and arrange follow-up soon.
